people.text: Jordan wants beginner-friendly cooking activities with clear explanations and practical support.
Riley enjoys social evening activities connected with cooking and meeting new people.
Sam prefers calm organised sessions with patient instructors and small groups.
Charlie needs inexpensive activities near buses or trains because they travel after work.
Avery likes creative projects and wants detailed feedback from experienced teachers.

texts.text: This intensive programme is only suitable for experienced users.
Groups are small and sessions are [8]carefully organised[/8]. Instructors give [8]patient step-by-step guidance[/8] to nervous newcomers.
Advanced participants complete independent homework and prepare for competitions.
Weekend family events are designed mainly for young children and parents.
Experienced leaders provide [10]individual feedback[/10] and encourage participants to develop [10]creative personal ideas[/10].
This course introduces [6]basic cooking skills[/6] through practical exercises. Teachers provide [6]clear support for beginners[/6] and explain every stage carefully.
The venue offers [9]low-cost sessions[/9] near the main station, making it useful for [9]people travelling after work[/9].
Members meet [7]on weekday evenings[/7] and spend time chatting before activities begin. It is popular with people interested in [7]meeting others socially[/7].

answers.explanation: Avery wants creativity and detailed feedback.
